description: IEEE-754 edge cases for RFC 8785 JSON Canonicalization Scheme. Expected canonical bytes are PROVISIONAL until cross-validated between @filen/rfc8785 and canonicaljson-go in Week 0 parity harness.

cases.name: zero positive
cases.rationale: baseline: integer zero is trivially canonical

cases.name: zero negative
cases.rationale: RFC 8785 requires -0.0 to canonicalize to 0 (no distinction between positive and negative zero in canonical form)

cases.name: epsilon above one
cases.rationale: smallest increment above 1.0 in IEEE-754 double; exercises shortest-representation algorithm

cases.name: pi double
cases.rationale: irrational with many significant digits; exercises round-trip precision

cases.name: tenth
cases.rationale: not exactly representable in IEEE-754; canonical form must use shortest roundtrip-safe representation

cases.name: sum floating error
cases.rationale: classic 0.1 + 0.2 floating-point result; many libraries truncate incorrectly

cases.name: max safe integer as float
cases.rationale: JavaScript Number.MAX_SAFE_INTEGER + 1; may be emitted as integer or float depending on library

cases.name: very small subnormal
cases.rationale: smallest positive double (Number.MIN_VALUE in JS); subnormal exponent edge case

cases.name: scientific notation normalization
cases.rationale: RFC 8785 requires canonical scientific notation above 1e21; implementations may differ on sign marker placement

cases.name: sub-one tenth
cases.rationale: canonical threshold for switching to decimal vs scientific; RFC requires decimal above 1e-6

cases.name: boundary e-7
cases.rationale: RFC 8785 boundary: values < 1e-6 use scientific notation
